text: Evidence is equivocal on whether iron supplementation improves athletic performance in iron-deficient nonanemic athletes, with half of reviewed studies showing benefit and half showing none.
Iron supplementation is more likely to improve performance in athletes with ferritin levels below 20 μg/L compared to those with higher baseline ferritin.
All studies demonstrating performance improvement from iron supplementation in nonanemic athletes used oral iron rather than intravenous administration.
A ferritin level of 20 μg/L may serve as a clinically useful threshold for deciding whether to supplement iron-deficient nonanemic athletes.
Iron deficiency without anemia is prevalent enough in endurance athletes that supplementation has become a common practice in endurance sports.
